about.title: About Michael Grife and the Boca Raton Injury Law Podcast
about.description: Michael Grife created Boca Raton Injury Law to help Florida accident victims understand their rights before, during, and after a personal injury or medical malpractice case. As the founder of The Grife Law Firm, Michael has spent his career representing people injured in car crashes, truck crashes, motorcycle crashes, medical negligence, nursing home neglect, and dangerous property fall cases. The firm has recovered over $100 million for clients across the state of Florida. Through the podcast, Michael breaks down how Florida law actually works — from the contingency fee structure and why it matters, to how cases are investigated, litigated, and taken to trial — so listeners can make informed decisions at the most vulnerable moments of their lives.
highlights: Unfiltered conversations on Florida personal injury and medical malpractice law from a founding attorney
Learn how Florida's contingency fee arrangement, discovery, mediation, and trial process actually work
Hear the strategy Michael Grife uses in catastrophic injury, wrongful death, and medical malpractice cases
Understand why Michael sees Florida's lack of mandatory bodily injury insurance as the biggest change the state needs
Practical guidance on preserving evidence, dealing with insurance companies, and choosing the right attorney
Florida Bar Rule 4-7 attorney advertising — this podcast is educational, not legal advice

attorney.name: Michael K. Grife
attorney.title: Founder & Personal Injury Attorney
bio: Michael K. Grife is the founder of The Grife Law Firm, a Boca Raton personal injury and medical malpractice practice serving clients throughout Florida. Michael Grife established the firm in 2014 after building his career at personal injury firms in Delray Beach and Fort Lauderdale, and has since recovered over $100 million for injured clients across the state. He is admitted to the Florida Bar (#16583) and the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of Florida, and is recognized by Super Lawyers, Best Lawyers in America for personal injury and medical malpractice, AV-Preeminent by Martindale-Hubbell, and the Multi-Million Dollar Advocates Forum.

Michael Grife grew up in Brooklyn and attended Hofstra University on Long Island, majoring in print journalism, before following a friend's invitation to visit Fort Lauderdale and deciding to stay. He earned his J.D. from Nova Southeastern University School of Law and has practiced personal injury and medical malpractice in South Florida ever since. Michael Grife credits his New York upbringing — and his stepfather's practical career advice — with shaping an approach that prizes relating to every client as a person rather than a file number, and with giving him the storytelling instincts he now uses to present cases to juries across Florida.

Today Michael Grife focuses on catastrophic injury, wrongful death, and medical malpractice cases — traumatic brain injuries, anoxic brain injuries, paralysis, stroke misdiagnosis, surgical error, and nursing home neglect — alongside car, truck, motorcycle, and dangerous property fall matters. He serves on the Palm Beach County Bar Association Civil Practice Committee and the personal injury and wrongful death CLE committee, and The Grife Law Firm is frequently recognized on peer-review honor rolls. Past results do not guarantee a similar outcome.

patrick-knight.name: Patrick W. Knight
patrick-knight.title: Shareholder, Board-Certified Civil Trial Attorney

education.school: University of Miami School of Law

bio: Patrick W. Knight is a shareholder and board-certified civil trial attorney at The Grife Law Firm. He is Board Certified in Civil Trial Law by The Florida Bar — a credential held by fewer than one percent of Florida attorneys — and has taken over 170 cases to jury trial.

Earlier in his career, Patrick Knight represented defendants in personal injury matters before transitioning to plaintiff-side work. That courtroom experience on both sides of the aisle informs how he develops cases for The Grife Law Firm's injured clients, from medical malpractice to catastrophic car and truck crashes.
Patrick Knight has been recognized as one of the Ten Outstanding Young Americans by the Junior Chamber of Commerce and selected for the 40 Under 40 Outstanding Lawyers of Miami-Dade County.
memberships: Board Certified in Civil Trial Law (since 2005)

disclaimers.text: This podcast and website constitute attorney advertising under the Florida Rules of Professional Conduct, including Chapter 4-7 governing lawyer advertising.

Viewing this site, listening to the Boca Raton Injury Law podcast, or contacting The Grife Law Firm does not create an attorney-client relationship. An attorney-client relationship with The Grife Law Firm is formed only through a signed written fee agreement.

Prior results do not guarantee a similar outcome. Every case is different and must be evaluated on its own facts. Discussion of past verdicts or settlements on this site or podcast does not guarantee a prospective client will obtain the same or similar result.

Any testimonial or endorsement shown on this site reflects the experience of that individual client or peer, is shared with consent, and does not guarantee a similar outcome. Clients are not compensated for providing testimonials.

Michael K. Grife and Patrick W. Knight are licensed to practice law in the State of Florida. Information on this site and podcast addresses Florida law and is not legal advice in any other jurisdiction.

The Grife Law Firm represents personal injury and medical malpractice clients on a contingency-fee basis. No attorney's fees are charged unless a recovery is obtained. Clients may be responsible for case-related costs as set out in a written fee agreement, consistent with Florida Rule of Professional Conduct 4-1.5.

The Boca Raton Injury Law podcast is for general informational and educational purposes only. Nothing discussed on the show is legal advice. Listeners with specific legal questions should consult a qualified attorney about their individual situation.

content.heroTitle: Boca Raton Injury Law with Mike Grife
content.heroDescription: Join Michael Grife, founder of The Grife Law Firm, for candid conversations about personal injury and medical malpractice law in Florida. From contingency fees and insurance tactics to catastrophic injury strategy and Florida-specific procedure, each episode gives accident victims and their families the knowledge they need to protect themselves and pursue full compensation.
content.articleTitle: Real Answers for Florida Accident Victims
articleParagraphs: Boca Raton Injury Law with Mike Grife is the podcast Michael Grife created to close the gap between how personal injury and medical malpractice cases actually work and what most injured people are told about them. Drawing on more than $100 million recovered for clients since founding The Grife Law Firm in 2014, Michael tackles the questions accident victims and their families actually ask — what the contingency fee really covers, how Florida's mediation and trial process plays out, and what to do in the first hours after a car crash, a fall, or a suspected medical error.

Each conversation goes beyond surface-level legal talking points. Michael walks through the strategy The Grife Law Firm uses in catastrophic injury and wrongful death matters — from preserving black box data and 911 recordings to lining up life care planners and medical experts — and explains when a case settles in pre-suit, when it settles at mediation, and when it belongs in front of a Florida jury. The podcast also looks at how technology and AI are changing legal practice, and why Michael believes Florida still needs mandatory bodily injury insurance to protect its drivers.
content.featuredQuote: When you hire The Grife Law Firm on a contingency fee, we're partners in your case. We don't get paid a dollar unless we win, and that aligns our interests with yours completely.

episode.title: The YOU Interview with Michael Grife
episode.description: In this debut episode of Boca Raton Injury Law, founding attorney Michael Grife sits down with co-host Abby for an unfiltered conversation about his life, his law practice, and the values that drive The Grife Law Firm. Michael shares his Brooklyn origin story, how a friend's Fort Lauderdale invite led him to Nova Southeastern University School of Law, why he built a practice around catastrophic injury and medical malpractice, how the firm's contingency fee structure actually works, and why Florida needs mandatory bodily injury insurance.

episodes.title: The YOU Interview with Michael Grife
episodes.subtitle: Episode 1 • The Grife Law Firm
episodes.description: Michael Grife opens The Grife Law Firm's first podcast with an in-depth conversation about his Brooklyn upbringing, his path from Hofstra to Nova Southeastern University School of Law, and how he built a Boca Raton firm focused on catastrophic injury, medical malpractice, and wrongful death. Michael explains how the contingency fee really works, why evidence preservation in the first 48 hours often decides a case, how negotiation stacks up against litigation and jury trial in Florida, and why the state needs mandatory bodily injury insurance.

faqGroups.label: About the Podcast
faqGroups.description: Learn more about Boca Raton Injury Law with Michael Grife and what to expect from each episode.

questions.question: What topics does Michael Grife cover on Boca Raton Injury Law?
questions.answer: Michael Grife covers Florida personal injury and medical malpractice law, including car, truck, and motorcycle crashes, dangerous property fall cases, nursing home neglect, wrongful death, and catastrophic injury matters. Each episode explains how Florida procedure and insurance rules shape real cases and how accident victims can protect themselves from the moment an injury happens.

questions.question: How often are new episodes released?
questions.answer: The Grife Law Firm releases new episodes on a rolling basis as Michael Grife and guest attorneys record new conversations. Subscribe on your preferred podcast platform to be notified when a new Boca Raton Injury Law episode goes live.

questions.question: Is this podcast only for people who have been in an accident?
questions.answer: No. Boca Raton Injury Law is designed for Florida drivers, families, patients, and residents who want to understand how personal injury and medical malpractice cases actually work before they need a lawyer. The podcast is educational — not legal advice — and is useful for anyone who wants to be informed about Florida law.

questions.question: Can I submit questions for the podcast?
questions.answer: Yes. Listeners can reach The Grife Law Firm through thegrifelawfirm.com or on Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn at @thegrifelawfirm to submit questions or topic suggestions. Michael Grife regularly addresses listener questions on upcoming episodes.

faqGroups.label: Florida Legal Questions
faqGroups.description: Common questions about personal injury and medical malpractice cases in Boca Raton and across Florida.

questions.question: What should I do immediately after a car accident in Florida?
questions.answer: After ensuring everyone's safety and calling 911, document the scene with photos, exchange information with other drivers, and seek medical attention. Florida's no-fault insurance system requires you to seek treatment within 14 days of a crash to preserve your PIP benefits. Avoid giving a recorded statement to an insurance adjuster before speaking with an attorney. Michael Grife emphasizes calling The Grife Law Firm quickly so evidence — including black box data and surveillance footage — can be preserved before it is altered or destroyed.

questions.question: How long do I have to file a personal injury claim in Florida?
questions.answer: Florida generally imposes a two-year statute of limitations on personal injury claims arising from negligence, including most car accident cases, under Florida Statutes Section 95.11. Medical malpractice, wrongful death, and claims against government entities have their own deadlines and pre-suit requirements. Michael Grife advises contacting an attorney as soon as possible so deadlines and evidence are protected.

questions.question: How does The Grife Law Firm's contingency fee work?
questions.answer: The Grife Law Firm takes personal injury and medical malpractice cases on a contingency fee basis consistent with Florida Rule of Professional Conduct 4-1.5. Clients pay no attorney's fees up front and no fees at all unless the firm recovers compensation. The firm advances case costs — filing fees, depositions, expert witnesses — and is reimbursed from any recovery. The written fee agreement spells out exact percentages and cost handling.

questions.question: What if the at-fault driver in Florida doesn't have insurance?
questions.answer: Florida is one of only two states without mandatory bodily injury insurance, which means many at-fault drivers carry only PIP and property damage. Uninsured motorist (UM) and underinsured motorist (UIM) coverage on your own auto policy is often the most reliable path to compensation. Michael Grife urges every Florida driver to carry UM/UIM coverage and discusses this issue in detail on the podcast.

footer.description: Boca Raton Injury Law with Mike Grife is a podcast dedicated to helping Florida accident victims understand their legal rights and navigate the personal injury and medical malpractice claims process. Hosted by Michael K. Grife of The Grife Law Firm, each episode delivers practical guidance drawn from more than $100 million recovered for injured clients across Florida.

topicalEntryGrid.title: Explore The Grife Law Firm's Practice Areas
topicalEntryGrid.description: Browse the Florida injury topics Michael Grife covers on the podcast. Each card links to a deeper resource from The Grife Law Firm on that situation.

links.title: Car Accident Lawyers
links.description: The Grife Law Firm represents Florida drivers and passengers injured in car crashes, from I-95 collisions to intersection impacts across Palm Beach County.

links.title: Truck Accident Lawyers
links.description: Commercial truck crashes involve federal trucking regulations, multiple liable parties, and catastrophic injuries — Michael Grife has handled Florida tractor-trailer cases for more than 15 years.

links.title: Motorcycle Accident Lawyers
links.description: Florida motorcycle crashes require aggressive investigation because insurers assume rider fault. The Grife Law Firm pushes back with scene reconstruction and expert analysis.

links.title: Bicycle & Pedestrian Accidents
links.description: Bicycle and pedestrian cases frequently involve dangerous sidewalks or distracted drivers. The Grife Law Firm has recovered millions in these matters, including a $3M sidewalk-hazard bicycle case.

links.title: Stroke & Heart Attack Misdiagnosis
links.description: Missed stroke and cardiac diagnoses are some of the most devastating Florida medical malpractice cases. Michael Grife has recovered seven and eight figures in stroke misdiagnosis matters.

links.title: Surgical Errors
links.description: Wrong-site surgery, retained instruments, and anesthesia errors at Palm Beach County hospitals and surgical centers. The Grife Law Firm works with medical experts to prove the standard-of-care violation.

links.title: Brain Injury from Medical Negligence
links.description: Anoxic and traumatic brain injuries caused by medical error require life care planning and lifetime damages models. The Grife Law Firm has secured eight-figure recoveries in these cases.

links.title: Nursing Home Neglect
links.description: Falls, bedsores, medication errors, and elopement in Florida nursing homes and assisted living facilities. Michael Grife holds operators accountable under Florida's nursing home statutes.

links.title: Slip and Fall Cases
links.description: Grocery stores, restaurants, and commercial properties. The Grife Law Firm moves quickly to preserve surveillance footage before it is destroyed.

links.title: Trip and Fall Cases
links.description: Broken pavement, uneven thresholds, and dangerous staircases. Michael Grife has recovered a $2M trip-and-fall verdict in a condo parking lot case.

links.title: Negligent Security
links.description: Assaults in hotels, apartment complexes, and parking garages. The Grife Law Firm pursues property owners who fail to maintain reasonable security in foreseeable-crime cases.

links.title: Cruise Ship Accidents
links.description: Cruise cases involve strict one-year notice requirements and Miami-based federal venue. The Grife Law Firm handles these matters across all major Florida ports.

links.title: Florida Wrongful Death Act
links.description: Who can file a wrongful death claim in Florida, the categories of recoverable damages, and how survivors are defined under Florida Statutes Chapter 768.

links.title: Medical Malpractice Wrongful Death
links.description: Wrongful death caused by missed diagnosis, surgical error, or delayed treatment. Michael Grife has secured seven-figure results in fatal medical negligence cases.

links.title: Fatal Car and Truck Crashes
links.description: Fatal crash cases often involve disputed liability, multiple insurance layers, and federal trucking rules. The Grife Law Firm handles both the claim and the grief with care.

links.title: Catastrophic Brain Injury
links.description: Anoxic and traumatic brain injuries that do not cause immediate death but permanently alter a family's life. Eight-figure recoveries are possible with the right experts and life care planning.

links.title: Florida No-Fault / PIP
links.description: How Florida's no-fault system works, the 14-day rule for seeking treatment, and when your claim can step outside PIP into a bodily injury liability action.

links.title: Uninsured / Underinsured Motorist
links.description: Florida is one of only two states without mandatory bodily injury insurance. Michael Grife explains why every Florida driver should carry UM/UIM coverage.

links.title: Case Results
links.description: See the verdicts and settlements The Grife Law Firm has secured for clients across Florida — including $10M, $8.5M, $3.5M, $3M, and $2.9M recoveries.

links.title: Free Consultation
links.description: Call The Grife Law Firm at (561) 998-0770 or toll-free (855) 998-0770. Consultations are free and no fee is owed unless there is a recovery.
